title: Diagnose and fix load error preventing game initialization

requirements.summary: Add detailed error tracking to capture and log the specific load error occurring on deployment, including stack traces, component mount failures, and any React Three Fiber or Physics initialization errors
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs clearly identify the source of the load error
Error messages include component names and lifecycle stage where failure occurs
Stack traces are preserved and logged for debugging

file_operations.description: Add comprehensive error tracking and logging throughout the component lifecycle, including Canvas initialization, Physics provider setup, and all child component mounting. Wrap critical initialization blocks in try-catch statements that capture and log full stack traces with component context. Add console logging for each lifecycle stage (pre-Canvas, Canvas mounting, Physics initialization, Player mounting, Arena mounting) to identify exactly where failures occur.

Add error boundaries and logging around physics body initialization, camera setup, and raycasting operations. Log all initialization parameters and any exceptions with full stack traces to identify player-specific loading failures.

Add error tracking for physics body creation, texture loading, and enemy instantiation. Wrap each physics object creation and enemy component rendering in error handlers that log failures with component names and initialization state.

requirements.summary: Verify that all required dependencies for React Three Fiber Canvas and Physics provider are properly loaded before attempting to render the 3D scene
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas only renders after all Three.js dependencies are confirmed available
Physics provider initialization is validated before use
Loading screen displays until all critical dependencies are ready

file_operations.description: Create a new hook that validates the availability of React Three Fiber, Three.js core modules, and Cannon physics dependencies before game initialization. Return loading state and error information if dependencies are missing or failed to load.

Integrate the useDependencyCheck hook to validate all 3D dependencies before rendering Canvas. Keep LoadingScreen visible until dependencies are confirmed ready. Add conditional rendering logic that prevents Canvas from mounting until all checks pass.

requirements.summary: Add fallback rendering logic that displays a meaningful error message to the user if the 3D canvas fails to initialize, rather than showing a blank screen
acceptanceCriteria: User sees a clear error message explaining the load failure
Error message includes actionable steps (e.g., 'Please refresh the page')
Fallback UI renders even when 3D initialization fails

file_operations.description: Add a fallback UI state that renders when Canvas or Physics initialization fails. Display a user-friendly error message explaining the 3D graphics failed to load, with actionable steps like refreshing the page or checking WebGL support. Ensure this fallback UI renders even when all React Three Fiber components fail.

Create a dedicated fallback component that displays when 3D initialization fails. Include clear error messaging, a manual reload button, and optional diagnostic information (WebGL support, browser compatibility). Style consistently with the game theme using the existing CSS variables from frontend/src/index.css.

requirements.summary: Implement retry logic that automatically attempts to reinitialize the game if the initial load fails, with a maximum of 3 retry attempts before showing a permanent error state
acceptanceCriteria: Game automatically retries initialization on failure
User is informed of retry attempts through loading screen message
After 3 failed attempts, a permanent error message displays with manual reload option

file_operations.description: Create a hook that manages game initialization with automatic retry logic. Track retry attempts (max 3), retry delays, and initialization state. Expose retry count and error state to parent components for UI updates.

Integrate useGameInitialization hook to manage Canvas and Physics initialization with automatic retries. Update LoadingScreen to display current retry attempt number. After 3 failed attempts, transition to permanent error state and render CanvasErrorFallback component instead of retrying.
Extend LoadingScreen to accept and display retry attempt information when provided. Show messages like 'Initializing game... (Attempt 2 of 3)' to keep users informed during retry cycles.

requirements.summary: Ensure the ErrorBoundary component properly catches and displays errors from the Canvas, Physics provider, and all child 3D components with detailed error information
acceptanceCriteria: ErrorBoundary catches all React Three Fiber rendering errors
Error display includes component stack and error message
Reload button in ErrorBoundary successfully clears error state and reinitializes

file_operations.description: Enhance ErrorBoundary to capture and display React Three Fiber and Cannon physics errors with full component stack traces. Add structured error information display showing error message, component stack, and timestamp. Ensure the reload mechanism properly clears all cached state and reinitializes the game from scratch.

Add a key prop to the Canvas that changes when reinitializing after error, forcing a complete remount. Ensure error state is properly reset before retry attempts to allow ErrorBoundary to catch new errors on subsequent initialization attempts.
